programmes.show_description: The air ambulance teams cut short an appearance at a charity event to treat a dirtbiker who's crashed at speed. Another off-road motorbiker breaks his thigh bone in the Pennines.
The staff at St George's tackle one of their most dramatic and unusual emergencies. Rail worker Alan has been hit by a high-speed train and is at risk of losing his right arm.
Eleven-month-old Isabel is rushed to St George's after having a fit and becoming unconscious. And 22-year-old Felix is brought in with a self-inflicted stab wound.
The campers go wild at Mynydd Derwydd near Clocaenog Forest with a bushcraft skills workshop. But with next to no facilities, will 250 acres of stunning scenery win them over?
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
...Special: Five Paralympic stars take on the culinary competition. Can dressage champion Natasha Baker ride off with the prize with her winter warmers? (Ep1/5)
...Special: On day two, judoka Chris Skelley's menu confuses one guest, while his starter impresses another. But what do they think of his rendition of a karaoke classic? (Ep2/5)
...Special: Paralympic presenter Arthur Williams goes all out with an international menu featuring fondue from Switzerland, nasi goreng from Asia, and apple crumble from the UK. (Ep3/5)
...Special: Wheelchair basketball star Amy Conroy hosts a night fit for historical heroes. But will Einstein, Julius Caesar, Audrey Hepburn and a suffragette enjoy the beer pong? (Ep4/5)
...Special: Retired swimmer Liz Johnson serves up some of her personal favourites, on a night featuring fishy goings-on, bonding over leftovers and a kazoo competition. (Ep5/5)
Jo and Neil are starting a new life in Lecce, southern Italy, and want a property that's close to the beach with a guest annexe. Jasmine Harman shows them what their £200k can buy.
Ambulance dispatcher Helen and builder Andrew from Yorkshire hope to buy a holiday home for all the family to enjoy in their favourite part of Tenerife: Los Gigantes.
Danni Menzies helps John and Carol look for a holiday home on Tenerife. With a budget of £120k, can they find their dream place - and will they put in an offer?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Rachael and Tim can't agree on whether to stay in their Wandsworth semi or up sticks. Can Kirstie's plan give them more space, or will Phil's wow-factor homes win?
...Renovations: In Amersham, Rollo and Olivia have ambitious plans to convert a run-down 300-year-old barber's shop into their home. Can they make it work for 21st-century life?
Kim's looking for a holiday home on Spain's eastern Costa del Sol, ideally a stone's throw away from the beach. Can Leah Charles-King find the perfect place for £115k?
Whitney and Rose dream of having their own holiday home near La Zenia on Spain's Costa Blanca, where they can enjoy all-year-round warm weather. Ben Hillman shows them what £80k can buy.
John and Elleke Ross welcome their rivals to the Mevagissey Bay Hotel in Cornwall. Catherine and Andrew are dazzled by the decking but puzzled by a mysterious gap around their door.
Jay and Sarah Brewer host at Glebe House Cottages in Devon. At breakfast, their bakery baskets pass the taste test, but the menu leaves the guests wanting more.
Catherine and Andrew Watt host at their converted police station, the Kings Park Hotel in Nottinghamshire. John and Elleke get forensic with the furniture. Sarah feels boxed in by her room.
Sarah Smith welcomes her guests to The Dukes in Bedfordshire, where she's under pressure to deliver at breakfast, and some of the feedback is hard to stomach.
It's the day of reckoning for the B&Bers. Whose property will emerge from the thud and blunder of bitter recrimination as the best value for money?
Brand new series: At the Royal Melbourne Hospital, Dr Scott fears a semi-conscious pregnant woman could lose her baby and her life. And a student has been attacked with a machete.
This episode focuses on older patients who are often reluctant to get the help they need, and younger patients who are treated after very different accidents.
This episode explores freedom, independence and renewal. David, who's 27, has fallen 40 feet from a tree and landed head-first on concrete. Anne, who's 74, has chest pains.
Brand new series: At the Royal Melbourne Hospital, Dr Scott fears a semi-conscious pregnant woman could lose her baby and her life. And a student has been attacked with a machete.
This episode focuses on older patients who are often reluctant to get the help they need, and younger patients who are treated after very different accidents.
This episode explores freedom, independence and renewal. David, who's 27, has fallen 40 feet from a tree and landed head-first on concrete. Anne, who's 74, has chest pains.
The campers end their tour of North Wales at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey with oyster gin and an aquatic tour of Puffin Island, before they pick their perfect pitch.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet motocross fan Tom. Henry upcycles Porsche wheel rims into stools. Simon makes an extraordinary lamp from old copper piping.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Sussex, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet Bill and Annie, who have a penchant for Star Wars collectables and, most importantly - as far as Henry is concerned - motorbikes!
Jon and Julia Shorrock host at the four-star Derwent House B&B in West Sussex. Andy and Julian have concerns over their carpeted bathroom, and Peter can't get any hot water.
Peter and Sharron Sorsky welcome their rivals to their Liverpool home, Kirkby Park B&B. But the guests discover dust, hairs, stains and even a hole in the wall!
Andy and Julian Banner-Price host at the swanky Plas Dinas Country House in Eryri (Snowdonia), where they've allocated their guests the priciest rooms.
The final visit is to Annette McAnespie's Hazelwood Farm in Crake, Yorkshire, where she's lined up her idea of fun for her guests: a cycling tour. But not everyone is up for it...
It's been a week of waterworks, tall tales and breakfast scrambles, but who will end up with the prize? And will any of the B&Bers want to see each other again?
Brighton. Will medical editor and rapper Adam's abundant vegetables impress his guests as he serves a simple yet wholesome vegetarian menu? (Ep1/5)
Brighton. Copywriter Will is confident that his veggie menu will trump Adam's from the night before. But there are some fishy goings-on and Will's risotto is upstaged... (Ep2/5)
Brighton. Retired advertising sales director Gill hosts with a Middle Eastern menu and a fun dress code, on a night featuring unexpected revelations and some very honest feedback. (Ep3/5)
Brighton. Teacher and surfer Jo treats her guests to a tropical-themed evening featuring some hit-and-miss food, a dessert drama and a cheeky massage thrown in for good measure. (Ep4/5)
Brighton. Social worker Ruth serves up a Jewish-Irish fusion menu on a night featuring kitchen chaos, some unexpected veggies and a knicker-nicking, limelight-stealing cat. (Ep5/5)
The competition moves to France, where four sets of Brits compete to be crowned best value B&B. First host Lisanne Ashton hopes to impress with her rustic rural guest house, Les Chimeres.
In France, eccentric royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou provide an unusual mix of vinegar tasting and royal regalia at Le Manoir in the Dordogne.
In France, Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver host at Le Clos Rabelais. There are high kicks, low blows and some unfavourable feedback.
George and Linda offer their guests 'a little slice of England' at the Moulin de la Civiere in Brittany, where one guest goes potty for the Victorian loos.
It's the final day in France. Tensions run high as the B&Bers discover how much they've been paid and settle some scores, before the winner is revealed.
In Hertfordshire, Kirstie Allsopp helps an American buyer come to terms with the smaller scale of British homes. And Phil Spencer offers guidance to some first-time buyers.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...or List It: Anne-Marie and Paul bought a doer-upper but haven't done it up. Kirstie has a master plan, but when the roof guzzles the budget, will a turnkey temptation seduce?
...Clive and Kids: The goats get new coats in their stockings as Christmas comes to Anty John's. And the family enjoy the house's first festive fire in decades.
This episode focuses on patients who've found love in unexpected ways. Bob, who's 51, has fallen 20 feet from a ladder. Anthony, who's 68, is constantly burping.
This episode features an elderly couple injured by a motorbike, and an actor who has broken his shoulder while rushing for the train and is needed on stage the same night...
